cases.name: all_valid
steps: build a batch of [N] valid items indexed [0..batch_size-1] from the item template
record the pre-count of the target collection
POST the batch to the bulk endpoint
assert each item [N] returned 2xx
assert post-count minus pre-count equals the batch size

cases.name: mixed_207
steps: build the batch with valid items plus one missing-required item [i] and one wrong-type item [j]
record the pre-count
POST the batch to the bulk endpoint
assert the overall response is 207 Multi-Status
assert item [i] returned 400 naming the offending (missing required) field
assert item [j] returned 400 naming the offending (wrong type) field
assert the valid items each returned 2xx
assert post-count minus pre-count equals the valid count

cases.name: all_invalid
steps: build a batch where every item [N] is malformed
record the pre-count
POST the batch to the bulk endpoint
assert each item [N] returned 400 or the whole batch was rejected top-level
assert post-count minus pre-count equals 0

cases.name: empty
steps: POST an empty batch [] to the bulk endpoint
assert the documented empty-batch status
assert post-count minus pre-count equals 0

cases.name: single_item
steps: build a one-element batch containing item [0] from the item template
record the pre-count
POST the one-element batch to the bulk endpoint
assert item [0] returned 2xx
assert post-count minus pre-count equals 1

cases.name: duplicate_within_batch
steps: build a batch containing a duplicate pair [i] and [j] that violate the uniqueness rule against each other
record the pre-count
POST the batch to the bulk endpoint
assert exactly one of the pair returned 2xx and the other returned 409 Conflict
assert the net delta for the pair is 1

cases.name: oversize_reject
steps: build a batch of max+1 items exceeding the configured maximum batch size
record the pre-count
POST the batch to the bulk endpoint
assert the batch is rejected naming the size limit
assert post-count minus pre-count equals 0

cases.name: atomicity_rollback
steps: assert or enable the documented transactional (atomic) mode
build a batch of valid items plus one invalid item [k]
record the pre-count
POST the batch to the bulk endpoint
assert the whole batch failed atomically with no partial writes
assert post-count minus pre-count equals 0

cases.name: bulk_update
steps: build an update batch with valid items and invalid items at known indices
record the pre-state of the affected records
POST the update batch to the bulk update endpoint
assert per-item statuses for valid and invalid items
assert only the valid items changed state and invalid items were unchanged

cases.name: bulk_delete
steps: build a delete batch mixing existing and non-existing ids at known indices
record the pre-count
POST the delete batch to the bulk delete endpoint
assert per-item statuses for existing (deleted) and missing (reported) ids
assert post-count minus pre-count equals the count of successfully deleted ids
